message:
Add unified status overview with system posture, convergence, and remediation hints

Enhance the rch status command with a comprehensive unified response:

- Add SystemPosture enum (RemoteReady/Degraded/LocalOnly) computed from
  daemon worker health to give operators an at-a-glance system state
- Query repo convergence status endpoint and include in unified output
- Generate actionable remediation hints from worker states, convergence
  issues, and posture-level problems with reason codes and suggested actions
- Extend render_full_status display with posture badge, convergence summary
  section, remediation hints panel, and worker pressure details
- Add UnifiedStatusResponse with schema versioning for JSON output mode

New types in status_types.rs: SystemPosture, RemediationHint,
RepoConvergenceStatusFromApi, UnifiedStatusResponse, and helper functions
for generating remediations from worker/convergence signals.

Also updates issue tracker with closed status for several issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","title":"Define fail-open semantics for unresolved path dependencies","description":"Specify and implement precise decision matrix: when to attempt remote build, when to trigger convergence/sync, and when to fallback locally. Must avoid silent partial sync behavior that can yield misleading build outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision matrix explicitly defines when RCH must fail-open to local execution for unresolved path-dep states.
- Matrix covers timeout, policy-violation, unknown-state, and verifier-failure scenarios.
- Each decision path emits stable reason codes and operator remediation hints.
- Semantics guarantee no unsafe remote execution attempt when prerequisite confidence is insufficient.
- Unit/integration tests validate every matrix branch with deterministic assertions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeHare","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:03.849405866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T22:31:21.770331022Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T22:31:21.770312738Z","close_reason":"Completed: explicit fail-open decision matrix for unresolved path deps with reason-coded tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fail-open","path-deps","policy","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.194937422Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.394997678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.594874089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","title":"Add exhaustive unit tests for resolver/planner/hash edge cases","description":"Test cases: relative/absolute path deps, symlinked repos, missing manifests, cyclic references, duplicate crates, out-of-root paths, and hash invalidation behavior. Include detailed structured logs for failing fixture states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test suite covers resolver/planner/hash edge cases including empty closure, large closure, and alias collisions.
- Failure-path tests assert precise reason codes and no silent fallback ambiguities.
- Regression tests include fixtures for malformed manifests and stale closure fingerprints.
- Test harness outputs structured logs with scenario id, expected decision, and observed decision.
- CI wiring includes fast targeted suite and broader nightly edge-case sweep.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeHare","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.053231372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T22:31:29.203382312Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","tests","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.791102461Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:13.989623615Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.194158900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","title":"Add E2E scenarios for real cross-repo Cargo path dependencies","description":"Implement end-to-end scenarios for real cross-repo Cargo path dependencies (for example project A depending on project B under `/data/projects`) using the shared reliability harness. Validate successful remote builds after convergence and correct fail-open local fallback when dependencies are unavailable or unverifiable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenarios cover direct and transitive path dependencies, symlinked `/dp` alias usage, and out-of-root rejection behavior.
- Success-path scenarios verify deterministic remote build success with expected artifact sync.
- Failure-path scenarios verify fallback without local re-execution loops, with explicit reason codes.
- Logs include per-phase closure plan, verification results, worker decision, and fallback rationale.
- Scenarios are integrated into the shared E2E suite and runnable in CI/nightly tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.256310490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:02.603597667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:18.602567803Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:17:38.462792646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:18.806605035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.312412741Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test-pyramid sequencing tightened: cross-repo path-dependency E2E now depends on edge-case unit coverage (bd-vvmd.2.7) to catch resolver/planner/hash corner cases early and keep E2E diagnostics focused on orchestration-level failures.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","title":"Add command-classification regression suite to protect non-compilation UX","description":"Add a dedicated regression suite to ensure new reliability logic does not degrade command classification behavior or accidentally intercept commands that must stay local. Focus on preserving user expectations for pass-through behavior while still capturing intended compilation offload cases.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Unit tests cover positive/negative classification for cargo/gcc/clang/make/ninja/bun patterns, including watch/piped/redirected/backgrounded exclusions.\n- Regression tests explicitly assert local pass-through for non-compilation and package-management commands (including bun install/add/remove/link, bun run/build/dev/repl, bunx).\n- Hook-mode integration tests verify fail-open behavior when classifier/preflight timing budgets are exceeded or preconditions are ambiguous.\n- E2E scenarios confirm no unintended remote interception side effects on representative real workflows.\n- Structured logs include classifier rationale, confidence, decision path, and fallback reason codes for every scenario.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:55:04.705571391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T00:52:46.134713580Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","e2e","fail-open","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.129835548Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.329762751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.737484699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.533601438Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical user-trust guardrail: this regression suite prevents accidental interception of commands that must stay local. Coverage must include positive compilation detection, explicit non-compilation pass-through, timeout/ambiguity fail-open behavior, and reason-coded decision telemetry so operators can explain every intercept/fallback decision.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","title":"Add command-classification regression suite to protect non-compilation UX","description":"Add a dedicated regression suite to ensure new reliability logic does not degrade command classification behavior or accidentally intercept commands that must stay local. Focus on preserving user expectations for pass-through behavior while still capturing intended compilation offload cases.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Unit tests cover positive/negative classification for cargo/gcc/clang/make/ninja/bun patterns, including watch/piped/redirected/backgrounded exclusions.\n- Regression tests explicitly assert local pass-through for non-compilation and package-management commands (including bun install/add/remove/link, bun run/build/dev/repl, bunx).\n- Hook-mode integration tests verify fail-open behavior when classifier/preflight timing budgets are exceeded or preconditions are ambiguous.\n- E2E scenarios confirm no unintended remote interception side effects on representative real workflows.\n- Structured logs include classifier rationale, confidence, decision path, and fallback reason codes for every scenario.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:55:04.705571391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:14:06.804088661Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:14:06.804066659Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive regression suite added: 21 classification tests in patterns.rs (cargo, bun, C/C++, build systems, shell structures, compound commands, real-world workflows, structured details), 6 hook tests (timing budgets, fail-open, passthrough, structured logs). All tests pass. Pre-existing clippy warning in util.rs also fixed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","e2e","fail-open","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.129835548Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.329762751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.737484699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.533601438Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.2.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical user-trust guardrail: this regression suite prevents accidental interception of commands that must stay local. Coverage must include positive compilation detection, explicit non-compilation pass-through, timeout/ambiguity fail-open behavior, and reason-coded decision telemetry so operators can explain every intercept/fallback decision.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3","title":"Workstream: Fleet Repo Convergence via repo_updater Integration","description":"Purpose: integrate `/data/projects/repo_updater` so workers maintain the convex hull of repos required by active/local development graphs. Build a daemon-level convergence service that verifies required repos exist before builds, synchronizes drift safely, and provides operator-visible status/remediation. This reduces missing-repo failures and startup latency for cross-repo local development.

## Success Criteria
- Adapter contract and policy allowlist constrain convergence to trusted roots and deterministic APIs.
- Convergence state machine and selection prechecks prevent missing/stale repo build failures.
- Background drift reconciliation and operator repair flows are available and observable.
- Repo-convergence unit/integration/E2E coverage is complete and deterministic.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.627230907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.107266701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["rch","rch-rel-2026q1","repo-sync","repo_updater","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:11.793591119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.606944357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:21.810214112Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.098985483Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:36.037914260Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.107177544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration principle: use repo_updater as source-of-truth synchronizer for repo presence/freshness, while RCH owns scheduling-time policy decisions. This centralizes sync logic and minimizes custom drift-management code in RCH.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Operational model: workers should hold the convex hull of repos needed by active build demand, not an unbounded mirror of everything. Convergence must be bounded by timeouts/rate limits and policy allowlist, with drift visibility in status surfaces. Pre-build selection checks are required to avoid known-missing-repo failures. If convergence cannot complete safely in budget, fallback locally and surface precise remediation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","title":"Define repo_updater adapter contract (commands, JSON schema, errors)","description":"Design stable integration boundary with repo_updater/ru: required commands, expected JSON outputs, failure taxonomy, timeout/retry behavior, and idempotency guarantees. Include version compatibility strategy and fallback behavior if adapter unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter contract specifies command surface, JSON schema, timeout budgets, and retry semantics.
- Contract documents authentication, host validation, and trust boundaries for repo_updater interactions.
- Error mapping normalizes adapter failures into RCH taxonomy with remediation guidance.
- Mockable adapter interface supports deterministic unit/integration tests.
- Contract includes explicit versioning and compatibility expectations for future updates.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.457858732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18.292823228Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18.092230052Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented repo_updater adapter contract module with schema/version/trust/fallback/error mapping, mock adapter interface, docs, and e2e contract test script.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contract","integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"comments":[{"id":215,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Paused while triage elevates bd-vvmd.5.1 as highest-impact actionable pick from bv --robot-next.","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:57:49Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented in rch-common/src/repo_updater_contract.rs and wired via rch-common/src/lib.rs. Added docs/architecture/repo_updater_adapter_contract.md and tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh. Validation run: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy -p rch-common --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p rch-common repo_updater_contract_ -- --nocapture, tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh. Note: workspace-wide clippy still has pre-existing blocker in rch/src/commands/daemon.rs (items_after_test_module).","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:15:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","title":"Implement daemon RepoConvergence service (convex-hull repo set + drift detection)","description":"Create service that computes required repo hull for active builds, compares worker state, and drives convergence actions through the adapter. Must expose drift state and convergence outcomes for scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RepoConvergence service computes and tracks required repo convex-hull state per scheduling context.
- Service maintains explicit drift states with deterministic transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Convergence decisions are bounded by time/attempt budgets to preserve fail-open behavior.
- Service emits structured state-change events for observability and operator tooling.
- Integration tests validate healthy convergence, partial drift, auth failures, and stale-state recovery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.663944951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704331106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704256056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["convergence","daemon","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.599381364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.397218582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.484662133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RepoConvergence service should expose deterministic states (ready, drifting, converging, failed, stale) with timestamps and reason codes. Keep convergence idempotent and bounded per worker to avoid starvation. Include explicit separation between policy decisions and execution so dry-run/status surfaces can use the same core logic.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:24Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: wired additional repo_updater convergence command surface into runtime path in rch hook pre-sync flow. Added command-aware idempotency keys and execution sequencing: SyncDryRun (read-only preflight) -> SyncApply (mutating convergence) -> StatusNoFetch (post-apply snapshot), all with strict fail-open behavior on validation/transport/adapter errors. Added helper coverage tests: test_repo_updater_command_name_is_stable and test_build_repo_sync_idempotency_key_for_command_distinguishes_commands. Validation performed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:59:04Z"}]}
@@ -449,7 +449,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","title":"Implement reliability log/artifact redaction + retention governance","description":"Add explicit redaction and retention governance for reliability logs/artifacts so detailed diagnostics remain safe for real-world use. Prevent secret leakage while preserving high debugging value from structured test and runtime evidence.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Redaction pipeline removes or masks credentials/tokens/keys/PII across logs, status payloads, and captured artifacts.\n- Retention policy defines TTLs, storage tiers, and cleanup behavior for scenario bundles and replay artifacts.\n- Redaction rules are schema-aware and versioned to avoid silent coverage gaps.\n- Verification tests inject synthetic secrets and fail if any secret survives redaction in output artifacts.\n- Operational docs specify safe export/share procedures for incident bundles and CI artifacts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:55:04.731563170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14.751803025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","observability","rch-rel-2026q1","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:01.941317997Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:02.344532054Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:31.147418392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:02.143142302Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Security and operability objective: preserve high-value diagnostics while preventing credential/PII leakage in logs and artifacts. This bead must define enforceable redaction and retention policy, include synthetic-secret leak tests, and produce incident-bundle handling guidance that is safe for CI sharing and human handoff.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:17Z"},{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: redaction/retention governance now depends on schema contract testing (bd-vvmd.6.8) so rule evolution cannot bypass machine compatibility guarantees.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","title":"Add cross-project helper contract-drift compatibility suite","description":"Add a dedicated compatibility/contract-drift test program for integrations with repo_updater, storage_ballast_helper, and process_triage. Goal: catch upstream/downstream interface drift (schema, semantics, timeouts, error mapping, safety policy) before users experience flaky remote execution or opaque fallback behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract fixtures exist for each helper integration surface (request/response schema, timeout semantics, error taxonomy mapping, and safety-policy expectations).
- CI and nightly run compatibility checks against explicit version matrixes (minimum-supported, recommended/current, and latest-tested where available).
- On drift, failures include actionable structured diffs and reason-coded remediation guidance (component, version, field/behavior mismatch, expected vs observed).
- Tests validate runtime fallback semantics remain correct when helper contract mismatch is detected (fail-open for compilation execution path, fail-closed for security policy violations).
- Structured logs for every compatibility scenario include component id, version tuple, operation, decision path, and fallback/remediation code.
- End-to-end scenarios verify mixed helper-version environments across workers and prove deterministic operator diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:31.083326423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14.956506163Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compatibility","contracts","integration","observability","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:47.976031706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:48.175297193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:48.372255980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:48.569748858Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:48.772435841Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:49.167733910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:48.970978334Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:49.366932632Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:31.348917120Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":180,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: compatibility drift across repo_updater/storage_ballast_helper/process_triage is now an explicit reliability gate with structured diffs, version-matrix checks, and deterministic fallback validation so users are protected from silent integration breakage.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:50Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency tightened: compatibility suite now depends on reusable E2E harness (bd-vvmd.7.7) to ensure mixed-version helper scenarios use deterministic setup/verify/cleanup with full structured logging artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","title":"Add tracing spans and Prometheus metrics for new reliability services","description":"Instrument convergence, preflight verification, pressure remediation, and process triage with latency/outcome/error counters and cardinality-safe labels. Include metrics for local fallback reasons.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tracing spans cover end-to-end reliability decisions with consistent correlation identifiers.
- Prometheus metrics include latency/outcome/error dimensions with cardinality-safe labels.
- Instrumentation captures local fallback reasons and critical decision branch counts.
- Telemetry overhead is measured and remains within budget constraints.
- Tests validate metric/span emission for success, fallback, and failure scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeHare","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:08.397604099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T21:56:21.558575927Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T21:56:21.558143186Z","close_reason":"Completed: -{"id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","title":"Add CLI/JSON status surfaces with actionable remediation hints","description":"Expose operator-facing commands and machine responses summarizing repo drift, path-dep readiness, storage pressure, process/reliability debt, and fallback posture. Ensure stdout/stderr separation and hook-safe behavior are maintained.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Status surfaces expose convergence, path-dep readiness, disk risk, multi-signal worker health/debt, and fallback posture in one coherent model.
- JSON mode remains machine-parseable and versioned; human mode remains concise and actionable.
- Output respects stdout/stderr contracts and hook-safe behavior.
- Each non-healthy state includes explicit remediation next step(s) with reason-coded diagnostics.
- Unit/integration tests validate schema and content consistency across output modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:08.608118298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.905314700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","observability","rch-rel-2026q1","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:48.905248766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:30.329966999Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:30.536212014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:30.740811748Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:30.947533776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.078954787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":186,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: status surfaces now explicitly depend on convergence/storage/process/fail-open sources so operators receive complete multi-signal state instead of partial telemetry.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","title":"Update README/docs runbooks for new reliability architecture","description":"Document design intent, failure modes, remediation playbooks, and rollout strategy. Include concrete examples and command references so future maintainers can operate without recovering context from external planning notes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README and runbooks document architecture intent, failure modes, and operational playbooks end-to-end.
- Docs include concrete command examples for diagnosis, dry-run, repair, and rollback paths.
- Documentation is aligned with implemented error taxonomy and status schema.
- Runbook includes incident triage flowchart and escalation checkpoints.
- Docs validation step ensures examples remain accurate as CLI surfaces evolve.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:08.818412105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.348022158Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","observability","operations","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1qhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:08:03.729632543Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:49.319547012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:58:04.436791933Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:50.553449006Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.676315625Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.144181896Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.347953039Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:17.224648550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","title":"Codify SLO guardrails and regression checks for reliability features","description":"Define measurable guardrails (hook latency, convergence latency, failure-rate budgets) and integrate checks into validation flow. Ensure regressions are detected before release.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guardrails define measurable budgets for hook latency, convergence latency, fallback rate, and triage overhead.
- Regression checks run in CI/nightly and fail clearly when thresholds are exceeded.
- SLO definitions include measurement windows and error-budget consumption semantics.
- Alert criteria avoid noise through debouncing/hysteresis where appropriate.
- Reports identify top regressions with actionable attribution data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:09.024659545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:52.022416760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","quality","rch-rel-2026q1","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1qhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.766352173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.972945701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.558134995Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.274463671Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.472492504Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:36.665207807Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SLO expansion: include cancellation-path reliability/latency/fallback budgets and UX diagnosability signals from bd-1yt6 and bd-1qhj.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","title":"Add performance budget benchmarks for new reliability pipeline","description":"Implement benchmark and regression checks for preflight/convergence/triage/ballast flows with explicit user-facing performance budgets. Include both microbenchmarks and scenario-level benchmarks using reliability E2E suites, fault-injection runs, and long-duration soak results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook decision latency budgets are benchmarked with threshold enforcement.
- Worker-selection + preflight/convergence latency budgets are benchmarked under normal and degraded conditions.
- End-to-end overhead benchmarks include full reliability suite, fault-injection, and soak paths.
- Benchmark output includes clear regression deltas and top contributors for triage.
- CI/nightly gate fails on budget regressions unless explicit override policy is used.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.247230478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51.175215362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["budgets","observability","performance","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51.174973419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:29.242386090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:19.582196787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:19.793331867Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: new reliability logic adds control-plane overhead; explicit benchmark gates protect the core RCH latency budgets and prevent regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:31Z"}]}
